Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Brian L. Bloomking, a/k/a Brian Lewis Bloomking, a/k/a Brian Lewis King, an individual.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Henry Yandell, Esq. appeared for plaintiff American Express national Bank, f/k/a American Express Centurion Bank.
Robert Rebhan, Assistant Custodian of Records for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. Yandell and Rebhan appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Brian L. Bloomking, a/k/a Brian Lewis Bloomking, a/k/a Brian Lewis King, an individual.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Common Counts: Book Account/Account Stated; and Quantum Meriut-Reasonable Value, was filed on July 30, 2018, and personally served on defendant Brian L. Bloomking, a/k/a Brian Lewis Bloomking, a/k/a Brian Lewis King, an individual, on November 26, 2018.
Default was entered on January 10, 2019.
DOE defendants were dismissed on February 14, 2019.

Plaintiff American Express National Bank, f/k/a American Express Centurion Bank, submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Card Member Agreement......................................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Documents in Support of Entry of Default Judgment, Bates Numbers 13-11.....ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff American Express national Bank, f/k/a American Express Centurion Bank, and against the defaulted defendant Brian L. Bloomking, a/k/a Brian Lewis Bloomking, a/k/a Brian Lewis King, an individual, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $60,468.49.
Costs - $580.00.

Total - $61,048.49.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
